{"id":"smallhold-45j5","title":"Idempotency-Key header: hash, check, return existing post on retry","description":"On POST /api/v1/statuses, if Idempotency-Key header present: SHA-256 hash of (key + account_id), check idempotency_keys table. If row exists, return the existing post. Otherwise proceed with creation, store the key hash. Prune keys older than 24h in a background tokio task.

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to test Ivory's retry behavior — does it always send the same Idempotency-Key on retry? Does it change the key on a new post?

Test discipline:
- POST with key, succeed → key stored, post returned
- POST same key again → SAME post returned (identical Status object), no new post created
- POST different key with same content → NEW post created (Idempotency-Key is key, not content-hash)
- POST after 25h → key expired, new post allowed (pruning works)
- Two concurrent POSTs with same key (race condition) → only one post created

Defensive programming:
- Key hash must include account_id to prevent cross-account idempotency collisions
- Concurrent duplicate: use INSERT OR IGNORE + SELECT pattern (not check-then-insert race condition)
- Never return a 5xx if the idempotency lookup fails — degrade gracefully and create the post","acceptance_criteria":"Duplicate request with same key returns same post; new key creates new post; 25h expiry works; concurrent duplicates produce one post","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:34:14Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:34:14Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-3l1","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:29Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
